faqs.question: Why are silverfish so common in Rockaway Beach homes?
faqs.answer: Rockaway Beach's oceanfront location creates consistently high humidity levels, especially in bathrooms, basements, and poorly ventilated spaces. Silverfish are attracted to moisture and thrive in coastal environments, making them particularly prevalent in this beachside community. The salt air and seasonal moisture fluctuations provide ideal breeding conditions year-round.

faqs.question: Can silverfish cause damage to my Rockaway Beach home?
faqs.answer: Yes, silverfish feed on carbohydrates and proteins found in paper, glue, clothing, books, and wallpaper. In Rockaway Beach homes, they commonly damage stored items in basements and closets, vintage books and photographs, natural fiber clothing, and wallpaper in humid rooms. Our treatment protects your belongings and prevents ongoing damage to your property.
